data.title: Choice Hotel's domestic-based demographic earns double upgrade at Goldman
data.description:
To address the shifting geopolitical and economic landscape, coupled with negative read-across from the U.S. airline industry, Goldman Sachs has lowered its outlook on certain names within the U.S. lodging and timeshare industry and raised its assessment of those asset-light stocks that enjoy more global diversity and are less indexed to international tourism into the U.S.

Against this backdrop, Goldman analysts Lizzie Dove and Ryan Davis have downgraded Hyatt (H) to Sell, Marriott (MAR) and Hilton (HLT) to Neutral, while simultaneously issuing a double upgrade to Choice Hotels (NYSE:CHH) to Buy from Sell.

Across the travel industry, lodging stocks have sold off materially with Hilton (HLT), Marriott (MAR), and Hyatt (H) all down by double digits over the past two months. The trend has been amplified by U.S. trade policy that Goldman views as already detrimental to inbound travel trends. Accordingly, Dove and Davis lowered their U.S. RevPAR estimates by ~125 basis points for 2025.

During the last shock to the travel industry, hotels that maintained an asset light, fee-based model, and unloaded owned and leased hotels, as well as timeshares outperformed the other C-corps during COVID.

“The pandemic was a great proof point of the resiliency of these business models, with U.S. RevPAR down ~80%, yet cash flow was fairly resilient,” said Dove and Davis.

As one of the most defensive names in Goldman’s coverage, Choice Hotels (NYSE:CHH) will likely be less “significantly” impacted by the current economic backdrop than the others as the majority of its customers originate within the U.S., shielding the company from declines in international tourism.

On the other end, Goldman considers Hyatt (H) one of its most macro-sensitive stocks among lodging, downgrading the stock to Sell from Neutral.

“While we appreciate Hyatt’s shift to asset light fees over the last few years, we are slightly more cautious versus peers due to Hyatt’s higher mix of management contracts, China exposure and more limited embedded growth in the in-construction pipeline.”

Goldman also downgraded Hilton (HLT) and Marriott (MAR), but with a business model that remains “best in class,” significant cash flow per share, and resilient balance sheets, both were downgraded to Neutral in respect of a valuation that is “still too high.”

Ahead of Monday’s open, shares of Choice Hotels (NYSE:CHH) are up more than 3%, while Hilton (HLT), Hyatt (H), and Marriott (MAR) are all marginally lower.

data.title: Morgan Stanley closes private equity fund at $2.3B in commitments
data.description:
Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) Investment Management  ("MSIM") reported on Monday  the final close of its North Haven Private Equity Co-Investment Opportunities Fund III (PECO III) oversubscribed at its hard cap with ~$2.3B in total commitments.

PECO III offers private equity exposure through co-investments alongside best-in-class buyout managers operating primarily in the lower middle market. The fund is the successor to two dedicated co-investment funds — PECO I and PECO II — and the continuation of Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) Private Equity Solutions' 25-year history of executing co-investments, including almost $6B of commitments since its inception at Morgan Stanly in 1999.

Morgan Stanley (MS) stock rose 1.8% in Monday premarket trading.

data.title: Air travel slumps as Apollo points to a noticeable decline in airports traffic
data.description:
Air travel has dropped notably across the ten busiest U.S. airports over the past two months, according to Apollo Asset Management, as both consumer sentiment and business travel weaken. Daily arrival data since late February points to a consistent and rapid decline in traffic.

“This is likely a mix of declines in business travel, tourism, and government travel,” Torsten Sløk, Apollo’s chief economist stated. 

See the below chart that Apollo Asset Management provided:

Expanding off Apollo Asset Management’s data point, Seeking Alpha constructed a list of the top 15 U.S. airline carriers according to its quant metrics. See the list below sorted from low to high.

No. 15: JetBlue Airways (NASDAQ:JBLU), quant rating of 1.80.

No. 14: Surf Air Mobility (NYSE:SRFM), quant rating of 2.55.

No. 13: Allegiant Travel Company (NASDAQ:ALGT), quant rating of 2.62.

No. 12: Frontier Group Holdings (NASDAQ:ULCC), quant rating of 2.73.

No. 11: Jet.Ai (NASDAQ:JTAI), quant rating of 2.83. 

No. 10: Sun Country Airlines (NASDAQ:SNCY), quant rating of 2.83.

No. 9: Alaska Air Group (NYSE:ALK), quant rating of 2.88. 

No. 8: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL), quant rating of 2.94.

No. 7: Joby Aviation (NYSE:JOBY), quant rating of 2.99.

No. 6: American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL), quant rating of 3.09. 

No. 5: flyExclusive (NYSE:FLYX), quant rating of 3.15. 

No. 4: United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL), quant rating of 3.20. 

No. 3: Blade Air Mobility (NASDAQ:BLDE), quant rating of 3.30. 

No. 2: Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV), quant rating of 3.39. 

No. 1: SkyWest (NASDAQ:SKYW), quant rating of 4.28. 

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

Three airline exchange traded funds that may be worth analyzing in further detail are the U.S. Global Jets ETF (NYSEARCA:JETS), MAX Airlines 3X Leveraged ETN (JETU), and the MAX Airlines -3X Inverse Leveraged ETN (JETD).

data.title: UBS sees a tough road ahead for Stellantis
data.description:
UBS lowered its rating on Stellantis N.V. (NYSE:STLA) to Neutral after having the automobile stock slotted with a Buy rating. 

Analyst Patrick Hummel noted that the 25% U.S. auto tariffs introduced on April 2 hit Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) harder than Detroit peers because ~35% of the company's vehicles sold in the US are imported. He also sees Stellantis (STLA) having a harder time regaining market share in a likely shrinking U.S. market and warns of a high probability of losses in North America and negative free cash flow. 

"Without the perspective of a successful US turnaround, a core element to our Buy thesis no longer  exists. After sharp recent underperformance, we think STLA shares already discount this outcome," highlighted Hummel.   

UBS lowered its EPS forecast for Stellantis (STLA) in 2025 and 2026 by 50%, and warned the auto stock is not attractive to investors looking for cash returns in the foreseeable future. 

Shares of Stellantis (STLA) were up 1.5% in premarket trading on Monday. The auto stock is down more than 30% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: NewtekOne rises on ~$2M of estimated annual expense savings announcement
data.description:
NewtekOne (NASDAQ:NEWT) was rising after the bank holding company announced estimated annual expense savings of ~$2M on a recurring basis beginning in the second quarter.

Shares were +2.09% Monday pre-market to $10.25.

NewtekOne announced the termination of three office space leases covering roughly 52,000 sq. ft., reflecting the company's strategic shift to a mostly remote workforce.

data.title: Stock index futures gain after Trump exempts tech from reciprocal tariffs
data.description:
Stock index futures rode high on Monday, as President Donald Trump announced temporary tariff exemptions on smartphones, computers, and other electronics primarily imported from China.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +1.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +1.9%, and Dow futures (INDU) +1.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 6 basis points to 4.43%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 4 basis points to 3.92%.

Trump's administration on Friday exempted smartphones, computers, and other electronics mostly imported from China from reciprocal tariffs, providing major relief for American tech players.

Wall Street on Friday overcame a volatile morning trading session to push higher, ending a wild week on a positive note. Tariffs dominated headlines, with the latest being that the Trump administration had made “very good progress” on trade negotiations.

"Tariffs continued to dominate headlines over the weekend, starting with news late on Friday that smartphones, computers, and some other electronics would be exempt from U.S. reciprocal tariffs," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

Turning to the week ahead, while tariffs will remain the center of attention, investors will also be keeping an eye on incoming U.S. data with the March retail sales and industrial production prints on Wednesday, Sidorov added.

"Over the weekend, Trump partially retreated from the retreat, emphasizing that a 20% tax would apply and additional unspecified taxes on technology are coming. This erratic policy making leads to investors questioning the existence of a competent plan," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The week ahead will also see a slew of big companies reporting their earnings, including Bank of America (BAC), Netflix (NFLX), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and American Express (AXP).

The economic calendar is on the lighter side today, with mainly the Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic and Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin's speeches on tap.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Q1 strong earnings beat on gains in FICC, equities revenue (updated)
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) Q1 earnings and revenue significantly outperformed Wall Street consensus estimates, reflecting robust increases in the bank's fixed income, currency and commodities revenue as well as equity revenue. A lower provision for credit losses also helped.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $14.12, topping the average analyst estimate of $12.27, rose from $11.95 in Q4 2024 and $11.58 in Q1 2024.

Total revenue of $15.06B, beating the $14.7B consensus, increased from $13.9B in the prior quarter and $14.2B a year ago.

Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) stock rose 3.6% in Monday premarket trading. (Updated at 9:00 AM ET).

"The beat largely reflected better-than-expected equities sales and trading revenue and a lower than expected effective tax rate (adjusted pre-tax income was 4% above our estimate), partly offset by lower-than-forecast investment banking fees," said HSBC analyst Saul Martinez. (Added at 9:00 AM ET.)

Q1 net interest income of $2.90B, missing the Visible Alpha consensus of $3.30B, climbed from $2.35B in Q4 and $1.37B in last year's Q1.

Provision for credit losses of $287M, smaller than the Visible Alpha consensus of $412M, dropped from $351M in the previous quarter and $318M a year ago.

Total operating expenses of $9.13B rose from $8.26 B in Q4 and $8.66B in last year's Q1.

Annualized return on average common shareholders' equity was 16.9%, up 2.3 percentage points from Q4 2024 and 2.1 pp from Q1 2024.

Loans of $210B at March 31, 2025, grew from $196B at Dec. 31, 2024. Deposits of $471B increased from $433B at the end of Q4 2024.

Headcount totaled edged up to 46,600 at the end of Q1, compared with 46,500 at the end of Q4.

Global Banking & Market net revenue of $10.7B climbed 26% Q/Q and 10% Y/Y. Investment banking fees of $1.91B dipped 7% Q/Q and 8% Y/Y, reflecting lower net revenues in advisory compared with a strong prior year period, partly offset by higher net revenues in debt underwriting, primarily driven by asset-backed and investment-grade activity. FICC net revenue of $4.40B surged 61% Q/Q and increased 2% Y/Y. Equity revenue of $4.19B jumped 21% Q/Q and 27% Y/Y, primarily from significantly higher net revenues in equities intermediation (primarily reflecting significantly higher net revenues in derivatives) and in equities financing (primarily reflecting significantly higher net revenues in portfolio financing). 

Asset & Wealth Management net revenue of $3.68B dropped 22% Q/Q and 3% Y/Y; management and other fees totaled $2.70B, down 4 % Q/Q and up 10% Y/Y.

Platform Solutions net revenue of $676M edged up 1% Q/Q and dipped 3% Y/Y.

"Our strong results this quarter have demonstrated that in times of great uncertainty, clients turn to Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) for execution and insight," said Chairman and CEO David Solomon.

Conference call at 9:30 AM ET.

Earlier, Goldman Sachs GAAP EPS of $14.12 beats by $1.85, revenue of $15.06B beats by $350M

data.title: Amentum secures $247M global counter-threat finance contract
data.description:
• Amentum (NYSE:AMTM) on Monday said it has secured a $247.6 million contract from the U.S. Department of Defense Air Combat Command (ACC), Acquisition Management and Integration Center (AMIC), Counternarcotics and Global Threats (CN&GT) Division.
  • Under this contract, Amentum will deliver solutions to the DOD in the counter-transnational organized crime, counter-drug, and counter-terrorism missions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cricut downgraded at Citi as Citi prefers AI networking stocks
data.description:
• Cricut (NASDAQ:CRCT) was downgraded to Sell at Citi on Monday as the investment firm said it prefers artificial intelligence-exposed stocks in the communications equipment and hardware space.
  • “We expect a rally in the group on Trump administration reciprocal tariff exemptions on PCs, smartphones and 20 other products announced over the weekend from the 125% tariff on Chinese imports and 10% baseline tariff on imports for other countries, while the 20% tariff on all Chinese goods remains in effect,” analysts at the firm wrote in a note to clients. "That said, we lower our data center capex & PC models ~5%/2% to reflect macro induced weak demand correlated with Citi’s in-house ~70bps global GDP contraction view in 2025.”
  • In addition to the Cricut downgrade, Citi said its top picks in the communications equipment and hardware space are Cisco (NASDAQ:CSCO), Amphenol (APH) and Coherent (COHR).
